macro_segments.title: Introduction to Hasokher et Haumnin: Employment Termination and Worker Compensation

micro_segments.title: Overview of the Baraita on Employer and Employee Withdrawal
Three scenarios: Pre-arrival cancellation, mid-work termination, and davar haaved
Tosafot on garmi and opportunity cost when employer cancels before arrival

macro_segments.title: When Workers Show Up but Cannot Work: Inspection of Land and Employer Negligence

micro_segments.title: Gemara on Rav Nasan and the baraita regarding full payment when no work available
Rav's objection and Rashi vs. Rambam on land inspection before hiring
Rava on irrigation ditches: responsibility based on anticipation and foreseeability
Work completed in half the day: assigning equivalent remaining work
Exception for achlushei Mechuza and muscular maintenance work

macro_segments.title: Workers Leaving Mid-Job: Tannaitic Debate on Pay Deduction and Loss Recovery

micro_segments.title: First opinion: workers get pro-rated pay with no consequences (Tanna Kamma)
Workers threatening to leave and employer persuading them to stay
Interpretation of employer's promise when convinced workers to remain
Rabbi Dosa's position: pay can be docked to cover replacement costs
Scenario where price declines and workers claim original agreement
Rav Huna's case: workers gave discount based on underpriced labor

macro_segments.title: Rav's Ruling on Worker Freedom to Withdraw and Reconciliation with Rabbi Dosa

micro_segments.title: Rav: worker can withdraw even mid-day based on principle of servant to God
Potential distinction between daily laborers and contractors
Baraita on emergency situations: death in family or illness
Gemara analysis: whose opinion governs the emergency baraita

macro_segments.title: Land Sale and Retraction: Upper and Lower Hand in Buyer-Seller Disputes

micro_segments.title: Mishna: whoever retracts has lower hand principle explained through land sale example

macro_segments.title: Rav's Ruling on Worker Freedom to Withdraw and Reconciliation with Rabbi Dosa (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara resolution: Rav splits position between contractor and day laborer

macro_segments.title: Land Sale and Retraction: Upper and Lower Hand in Buyer-Seller Disputes (continued)

micro_segments.title: Seller retracts: buyer chooses payment form and quality of land
Buyer retracts: seller chooses payment form and quality of land
Rabban Shimon Gamliel's solution: structured payment makes deal final

topical_tags.term: yad poel al ha'elyona (worker has upper hand)
yad poel al hatachtona (worker has lower hand)
